steps.title: Find the growth multiplier
steps.content:
Increasing $x$ by $1$ changes the function from $f(x)$ to $f(x+1)$:
$$f(x+1)=18b^{x+1}=b(18b^x)=bf(x)$$

steps.title: Connect multiplier to percent
steps.content:
An increase of $e\%$ gives multiplier
$$1+\frac{e}{100}$$
Since the multiplier is $b$, set
$$1+\frac{e}{100}=b$$

steps.title: Solve for the percent increase
steps.content:
Subtract $1$ and multiply by $100$:
$$\frac{e}{100}=b-1$$
$$e=100(b-1)$$
The answer is D.
